title: React State Management – useState, useReducer & More

meta.title: React State Management – Complete Guide (2026)
meta.description: Master state management in React. Learn useState, useReducer, Context API, and advanced patterns for managing application state effectively.

content.value: State is data that changes over time and affects what gets rendered on the screen. Unlike props which are passed from parent to child, state is internal to a component and can be updated using setter functions.

value: **Never mutate state directly** – Always use setter functions
**Use functional updates** when new state depends on previous state
**Batch related state** into a single object when updates are related
**Keep state minimal** – Derive values when possible
**Lift state up** when multiple components need access
**Memoize selectors** with useMemo to prevent unnecessary recalculations

content.value: React provides multiple tools for state management. Start with useState for local state, useReducer for complex logic, and Context API for global state. For large applications, consider dedicated state management libraries like Zustand or Redux.

faq.question: useState vs useReducer?
faq.answer: useState is simpler for independent values; useReducer is better for complex state logic with multiple sub-values.

faq.question: When to use Context API?
faq.answer: For global state that doesn't change frequently, like theme, user auth, or language settings.

faq.question: Why not mutate state directly?
faq.answer: React relies on immutable updates to detect changes and trigger re-renders.

faq.question: What is state lifting?
faq.answer: Moving shared state to the closest common ancestor component.

quiz.question: How do you correctly update object state?
Which hook is best for complex state logic?
What is the purpose of functional updates?
When should you lift state up?
options: When multiple components need same state
